steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a disadvantage of conventional thermal preservation methods. The passage contrasts infrared heating with conventional methods, so look for what conventional methods do worse. The passage states infrared heating improves on conventional methods by "reducing industrial cooking times" — meaning conventional methods have longer cooking times.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — They are especially vulnerable to technological breakdowns.: The passage never mentions anything about equipment failure or reliability. This is invented. ✗
  • Choice B — They are only effective in heating foods that are lightweight and small in size.: The passage says conventional methods heat large batches — there is no mention of size limitations. This is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — Their cooking times are longer than those of other methods.: The passage explicitly states that infrared heating works by "reducing industrial cooking times" compared to conventional methods. If infrared reduces cooking times, conventional methods must take longer — a direct textual inference. ✓
  • Choice D — They require more energy than other methods do.: The passage does not discuss energy consumption for either method. This is invented. ✗
That matches Choice C — Their cooking times are longer than those of other methods..
